title: GitHub AgentOS Spec
description: Schema for agentOS.yaml — the portable configuration file for label-driven multi-agent orchestration on GitHub.

specVersion.description: Semantic version of this spec file. Bump minor for backwards-compatible additions; major for breaking changes.

specId.description: Stable identifier for this spec. Used for dependency pinning.

agents.description: Agent role definitions. Each role maps to a GitHub App identity and a set of status labels that trigger it.

id.description: Unique role identifier. Used as label suffix (agent:{id}), branch prefix, env var name.

create_app.description: If true, bootstrap registers a dedicated GitHub App for this role.

reuse_app.description: ID of another agent whose GitHub App credentials this role shares. Mutually exclusive with create_app: true.

permissions.description: GitHub App permission scopes. Values: read | write.

triggers.description: Status labels that cause this agent to be dispatched. Format: '{axis}:{value}'.

branch_prefix.description: Git branch prefix for this role. Empty string means role does not open branches.

runner.description: Shell command used to invoke the agent. Receives env vars per SPEC.md §5.

max_turns_default.description: Default max turns if not overridden by board field.

branch_convention.description: Branch naming pattern. Must contain {role} and {issue_number}.

receipt_comment_marker.description: HTML comment marker used to identify run receipt comments.

governance.description: Planning stage, approval gate, and provenance-weighted governance (SPEC.md §3.6, §12.9).

planning.description: Whether a plan and approval are required before the builder runs.

approvers.description: GitHub collaborator permission levels whose approval commands authorise a build.

changes_command.description: Slash-command that sends an issue back to status:plan.

wild.description: Stricter downstream defaults for issues carrying source:wild (SPEC.md §12.9).

auto_merge.description: Whether wild PRs may auto-merge. False regardless of repo default.

final_approval.description: Who performs the final merge for wild work. v1.2 permits only 'human'.

max_review_cycles.description: Review cycles before escalation; tighter than the planned-lane default.

changes_requested_routes_to.description: Whether an agent iterates on wild code after review, or the review is posted for a human push.

intake.description: The unplanned ('wild') PR pipeline: classification, stub issues, janitors, the archaeologist, and /approve-intent (SPEC.md §12).

exclude_actors.description: PR authors ignored by intake entirely (bot flood guard).

min_permission.description: Trusted-author gate (SPEC.md §12.2.7). Intake runs only for authors whose repo collaborator permission is at/above this tier, or who appear in allow_actors. Untrusted authors' PRs are left untouched (no stub, no LLM spend). Fails closed on misconfiguration.

allow_actors.description: Logins always trusted by the intake gate regardless of their collaborator permission tier (SPEC.md §12.2.7).

linked_prs.description: Treatment of human PRs that have a closing-linked issue.

settle_seconds.description: Quiet period (no author pushes) before autofix janitors and the archaeologist run.

convergence_passes.description: Max full janitor-sequence passes before declaring non-convergence.

test_oracle.description: Run the repo test suite before/after autofix as a behavioural oracle.

max_diff_lines.description: Above this diff size, skip interpretation: facts-only reconstruction.

max_recon_runs.description: Max archaeologist runs per stub; beyond it, facts-only mode.

model.description: Archaeologist model override; null = orchestrator default.

approve_intent_self.description: May the PR author approve their own intent (if they hold the authoriser role)?

tripwire_paths.description: Deterministic path rules; any diff match routes the stub to status:blocked before any agent runs.

janitors.description: Janitor sequence, run serially in declared order (SPEC.md §12.5).

run.description: Shell command that runs this janitor tool.

mode.description: autofix pushes commits; report emits check runs and findings only.

block_on.description: Route the stub to status:blocked when findings at or above this severity exist. Omit to never block on this janitor's findings.

paths.description: Per-path janitor mode ratchet. Glob keys map to a mode override for files matching that path. Example: 'legacy/**': {janitors_mode: report} keeps legacy code report-only even if the global mode is autofix.

autofix.description: Whether autofix janitors run on fork PRs. Always false per spec; not configurable upward.

reports.description: Report-to-work: how report findings become follow-on issues at settlement (SPEC.md §12.8.4).

file.description: Whether to file an issue for this category at settlement.

start.description: auto applies status:todo on the spawned issue; manual leaves launch timing to a human.

labels.description: Label axis definitions. Each axis groups related labels under a shared prefix.

name.description: Label value. Full label name will be '{axis}:{name}'.

description.description: Human-readable description shown in GitHub UI.

routes_to.description: Agent role ID that is triggered when this label is applied. Null = informational only.

auto_add_to_board.description: If true, issues created with this label are automatically added to the Projects v2 board.

flow.description: metadata: set before/during run. parameter: configures run. telemetry: written by runner.

label_map_axis.description: If set, this field is synced from the named label axis. The label value is mapped to an option name.

source.description: Who writes this field. Only valid for telemetry fields.

reduce.description: How to aggregate values across multiple runs.

schema_version.description: Run-record schema version. Increment when adding required fields.

output_path.description: Directory for JSONL corpus files, relative to repo root.

retention_months.description: How many months of data to keep in active JSONL files.

required_fields.description: Dot-notation paths that must be present in every run record.

plugins.description: Optional plugins applied after core provisioning. Plugins add labels, fields, workflows. They MUST NOT modify core resources.
